Description: The bedrock of motion design, controlling the speed and rhythm of elements.

SubPrinciples.Effect: Finding the balance between 'too fast to notice' and 'too slow to wait for'.
Simulating physical inertia to make movement feel natural and intentional.
Maintaining a consistent feel across all interactions within a system.

Description: Ensuring users understand where elements come from and where they go.

SubPrinciples.Effect: Creating a sense of depth and physical space between layers.
Motion that originates from the point of user contact or action.
Using motion to suggest where more content exists (e.g., right to left).

Description: Using motion to guide the user's attention to the most important elements.

SubPrinciples.Effect: Revealing content in a logical order to prevent cognitive overload.
Drawing the eye to critical updates, errors, or next actions.
Morphing existing elements to show relationship between old and new state.

Description: Infusing brand personality and emotional resonance into the interface.

SubPrinciples.Effect: Preparing the user for an action with a subtle reverse movement.
Adding 'squash and stretch' physics to emphasize energy and playfulness.
Elements reacting to the proximity of the user's cursor or touch.

Description: Using motion to communicate system status and interaction confirmation.

SubPrinciples.Effect: Providing visual weight to digital interactions to feel tactile.
Communicating success or failure through motion patterns.
Showing the limits of scroll or movement through rubber-banding.

Description: Ensuring digital elements behave according to consistent physical laws.

SubPrinciples.Effect: Heavier elements moving slower and requiring more distance to stop.
Digital materials bending or stretching on impact or interaction.

Description: Using motion to guide the user through a logical sequence or story.

SubPrinciples.Effect: Elements appearing only when relevant to the current user task.
Multiple elements moving in synchronized harmony to establish context.

Description: Designing motion that respects user needs and system preferences.

SubPrinciples.Effect: Replacing intense animations with subtle fades for sensitive users.
Ensuring motion doesn't distract or confuse users from their main goal.

Description: Handling the change of form and value within elements.

SubPrinciples.Effect: Animating numbers or status labels to reflect continuous change.
Using shapes to reveal or hide content layers strategically.
Creating new elements from existing ones to show relationship.

Description: Using movement through the Z and X/Y axis to explore content.

SubPrinciples.Effect: Moving the 'camera' closer to or further from UI elements.
Layers moving at different speeds to establish structural hierarchy.
Elements sliding over others to establish temporary focus.
